questions.question: What does 'npm init' do?
options: B) Creates a new package.json file with project metadata

questions.question: What does 'npm init -y' do differently from 'npm init'?
options: B) It creates package.json with all default values, skipping prompts
C) It creates a yarn.lock file
D) It initializes a TypeScript project

questions.question: What is the purpose of package.json?
options: A) To store compiled JavaScript code
B) To define project metadata, dependencies, and scripts
C) To configure the TypeScript compiler
D) To list all files in the project

questions.question: What is the difference between 'dependencies' and 'devDependencies'?
options: B) dependencies are for production, devDependencies are only needed during development/building

questions.question: Which command installs a package as a devDependency?
Where should TypeScript (@types packages, vitest, etc.) be listed?
What does semantic versioning (semver) look like?
In semver 2.1.3, what does incrementing the MAJOR version indicate?
options: B) A new feature that is backward-compatible
C) A breaking change that may not be backward-compatible

questions.question: What does the caret (^) mean in '^2.1.3'?
options: B) Compatible with 2.x.x (allows minor and patch updates)

questions.question: What does the tilde (~) mean in '~2.1.3'?
options: A) Any version of the package
C) Approximately equivalent: allows patch updates only (2.1.x)

questions.question: What is the purpose of package-lock.json?
options: B) To lock the exact versions of all installed dependencies for reproducible builds
C) To prevent anyone from modifying package.json
D) To store npm login credentials

questions.question: Should package-lock.json be committed to version control?
options: A) No, it should always be in .gitignore
B) Yes, to ensure all team members get the same dependency versions
D) Only if using npm, not yarn

questions.question: What does 'npm run' do?
options: A) Runs the main entry point of the package
B) Executes a script defined in the 'scripts' section of package.json
C) Runs npm in interactive mode

questions.question:
Given this package.json scripts section:
"scripts": {
  "build": "tsc",
  "start": "node dist/index.js",
  "test": "vitest"
}

How do you run the build script?
Which npm scripts can be run without the 'run' keyword?
What does 'npx' do?
options: B) Executes a package binary without permanently installing it
C) Publishes a package to npm
D) Creates a new project from a template

questions.question: Where does npm install packages locally?
Should node_modules/ be committed to Git?
options: B) No, it should be in .gitignore and recreated via 'npm install'
D) Yes, but only for small projects

questions.question: What does 'npm install' (with no package name) do?
options: B) Installs all dependencies listed in package.json
C) Shows a list of available packages

questions.question: What does 'npm uninstall lodash' do?
options: A) Removes lodash from node_modules and package.json
B) Removes lodash from node_modules only

questions.question: What is the 'main' field in package.json used for?
options: A) Specifying the main development file
B) Defining the entry point when the package is imported by other modules
C) Setting the main test file

questions.question: What are @types/ packages (e.g., @types/node)?
options: B) TypeScript type declaration packages that provide types for JavaScript libraries

questions.question: What does 'npm outdated' show?
options: A) A list of deprecated packages
B) Packages that have newer versions available compared to what's installed
C) Packages that are no longer in the registry

questions.question: What is the difference between 'npm install -g' and 'npm install'?
options: A) -g installs a greater number of files
B) -g installs the package globally, available system-wide; without -g installs locally

questions.question: What field in package.json sets the project to use ES modules instead of CommonJS?
